title: CodexCrossCheck
description: maker≠checker external authority. IN-WORKFLOW this shape is ADVISORY (the codex-opponent agent cannot prove it actually shelled out to codex). The AUTHORITATIVE maker≠checker gate is guardrails/codex-crosscheck.sh, run by the calling session directly on the evidence files; its exit code — not this JSON — gates the real verdict. absent/unauthenticated codex → available:false → fail-closed to unverified.

available.description: false if codex CLI is missing/unauthenticated → harness fails closed to unverified.

termination_verdict.description: concur only if the current-intent queue is genuinely drained to fixpoint with live evidence; dissent if anything is undecided/unwired; unverified if it cannot adjudicate.

carves.description: Adjudication of proposed new-scope carves. Only concurred carves leave the queue; dissent keeps an item open (blocks laundering an in-scope residual out).
